content: Show collapsed section preview
reason: Users can see agent tasks at a glance without expanding sections

reason: updated_at changes every second for active agents, causing constant visual churn in the dashboard grid

content: Dashboard must be fully usable at 666px width (half MacBook Pro screen). No horizontal scrolling. All critical info visible without scrolling.
reason: Primary workflow is orchestrator CLI + dashboard side-by-side on MacBook Pro. Minimum width constraint - should expand gracefully on larger displays.

content: Usage progress bars show reset times inline with percentage
reason: Reset time context is essential for orchestrator to know when to switch accounts or wait

content: Check for displayable content (tldr, outcome, close_reason) before rendering synthesis section
reason: Prevents empty bordered sections from appearing when synthesis object exists but has no content

content: Use spawned_at for stable sort in both Active and Recent sections
reason: Prevents jostling when SSE updates modify updated_at; Archive can use volatile sort since historical recency matters there

content: Use beads close_reason as fallback when synthesis is null
reason: Light-tier agents don't create SYNTHESIS.md by design, but beads close_reason contains equivalent summary info

content: Monitor Vibium but stick with Playwright MCP
reason: Vibium (by Selenium creator) is promising but v1 too early (shipped Dec 22, 2024). Lacks JS eval, network monitoring, DOM inspection we need. Playwright MCP remains superior per .kb/investigations/simple/2025-12-02-side-side-comparison-browser-use.md. Revisit when Vibium v2 ships. GitHub: github.com/VibiumDev/vibium

content: orch-go auth implementation is complete
reason: Code review shows Go has OAuth login, refresh, switch, capacity tracking - all features from Python orch-cli

content: TokenRefreshError includes account name for actionable guidance
reason: Enables 'orch account remove X && orch account add X' suggestion when token refresh fails

content: Keep beads as external dependency with abstraction layer
reason: 7-command interface surface is narrow; dependency-first design (ready queue, dep graph) has no equivalent in alternatives; Phase 3 abstraction addresses API stability risk at low cost

content: BEADS_NO_DAEMON=1 in .zshrc only
result: Doesn't propagate to launchd agents or processes spawned before shell sourcing. Added to launchd plist as well.

content: bd exec.Command calls in orch-go are concurrency-safe
reason: Beads daemon serializes database access via Unix socket, GetCommentsBatch has semaphore limiting to 10 concurrent

content: pkg/beads uses direct RPC with CLI fallback
reason: Performance gains from skipping process spawn, graceful degradation when daemon unavailable

content: RPC-first fallback pattern for beads operations
reason: Sequential RPC calls through single connection are simpler than concurrent CLI subprocess spawning; daemon handles serialization so semaphore pattern is unnecessary

content: beads RPC migration pattern
reason: Try RPC client first, fall through to CLI on any error (socket not found, connect failed, operation failed) - graceful degradation

content: beads RPC client fallback pattern
reason: Try RPC client first, fallback to CLI on error - provides performance when daemon running, compatibility when not

content: Dashboard progressive disclosure is already fully implemented
reason: Active/Recent/Archive sections with 24h threshold, localStorage persistence, count badges, and preview text all exist in current codebase

content: Dashboard project filter follows skill filter pattern - state var, unique extraction, apply function, dropdown UI
reason: Consistent pattern makes future filter additions predictable and maintainable

content: Skill output verification parses skill.yaml directly
reason: skillc verify CLI doesn't exist, so we parse outputs.required from skill.yaml files in Go

content: KB context uses priority-based truncation: constraints > decisions > investigations
reason: Constraints are critical (must respect), decisions next (prior choices), investigations lowest (exploratory)

content: orch serve status uses health check not PID file
reason: Simpler, no state management needed, health check is authoritative for whether server is responding

content: Orchestrator is AI, not Dylan - Dylan interacts with AI orchestrators who spawn/complete agents
reason: Investigation 2025-12-25-design-orchestrator-completion-lifecycle-two incorrectly framed Dylan as the actor who spawns agents. The actor model is: Dylan ↔ AI Orchestrator ↔ Worker Agents. Mental model sync flows: Agent→Orchestrator (synthesis) and Orchestrator→Dylan (conversation).

content: Daemon completion polling preferred over SSE detection
reason: SSE busy->idle triggers false positives during normal agent operation; Phase: Complete is only reliable signal

content: SSE busy->idle cannot detect true agent completion
reason: Agents go idle during loading, thinking, waiting for tools - not just when done

content: Skill manifests should declare spawn requirements
reason: Makes skills self-contained and portable. Spawn reads manifest and honors declarations instead of hardcoding behavior.

content: Daemon completion uses beads polling not SSE
reason: SSE busy->idle detection has false positives during tool loading; Phase: Complete in beads comments is only reliable signal

content: Investigation filenames are generated from task description at spawn time
reason: generateSlug() runs at spawn before agent starts - cannot reflect findings in initial filename

content: Skills own domain behavior, spawn owns orchestration infrastructure
reason: Architect review found current separation is correct design, not fragmentation. Skills containing beads/phase logic would reduce portability and violate Compose Over Monolith. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-25-design-should-we-evolve-skills-where.md

content: Ask 'should we' before 'how do we' for strategic direction changes
reason: Epic orch-go-erdw was created assuming skills-as-value was correct direction. Architect review revealed the premise was wrong - current separation is intentional design. Wasted work avoided by validating premise before execution.

content: SHOULD-HOW-EXECUTE sequence for strategic questions
reason: Epic orch-go-erdw was created from 'how do we' without testing premise. Architect found premise wrong. Wasted work avoided if 'should we' asked first.

content: Complete agents before spawning more - maintain spawn:complete ratio
reason: Dec 25 reflection: 201 issues created vs 12 closed in 24h. Backlog grew faster than completion. 29 stuck agents accumulated. Spawn discipline prevents debt accumulation.

content: Pre-spawn context gathering (<5 min, for spawn prompts) is distinct from deep investigation (>15 min, delegate)
reason: Orchestrator skill conflated these; explicit boundary resolves contradiction. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-orchestrator-pre-spawn-context-gathering.md

content: Dashboard uses SYNTHESIS.md as fallback for untracked agent completion detection
reason: Untracked agents have fake beads IDs that won't match real issues, so Phase: Complete check fails - workspace-based detection is the reliable fallback

content: orch status uses GetIssuesBatch to check closed status
reason: Provides both task info and closed status in single batch call, replacing ListOpenIssues which only returned open issues

content: orch complete surfaces synthesis recommendations before closing
reason: Ensures investigation follow-up items aren't lost during agent completion

content: 'orch review done' processes completions
reason: Previously only printed status, now actually closes beads issues and cleans up tmux. Addresses completion loop gap.

content: orch review done command complete
reason: Already implemented in commit 635a7c1 (Dec 25 2025). The spawn context was based on stale investigation data. Feature verified working: closes beads issues, cleans up tmux, logs events.

content: orch complete prompts for each SYNTHESIS.md recommendation individually
reason: Better UX than batch y/n - allows selective follow-up issue creation

content: Visual verification gate is already integrated into VerifyCompletionFull
reason: Found that VerifyVisualVerificationForCompletion is called in check.go:394-403, blocking completion when web/ files modified without evidence

content: Daemon-first for batch work
reason: Label issues triage:ready + skill:X, daemon auto-spawns. Orchestrator focuses on triage, labeling, synthesis, completion review. Manual orch spawn for single urgent items only.

content: No sacred cows - everything is replaceable at the right cost
reason: Load-bearing (Provenance, Session Amnesia) requires equivalent replacement. Implementation (beads, orch, kn, kb) requires something better. Convention (paths, naming) requires friction < value. Nothing is immune from questioning.

content: Don't spawn multiple agents to edit the same file
reason: Causes merge conflicts and wasted work. Consolidate into one prompt or spawn sequentially.

content: OpenCode serve requires --port 4096 flag
reason: Default is random port. Daemon, orch CLI, and dashboard all expect 4096.

content: kn entries can accumulate into skill changes
reason: Pattern: multiple related constraints/decisions on same topic → skill update. Example: daemon kn entries → skill launchd docs + daemon-first workflow. Not yet automated - manual recognition required.

content: Pressure Visibility System has 3 layers: Gap Detection, Failure Surfacing, System Learning Loop
reason: Operationalizes Pressure Over Compensation principle - each layer builds on previous

content: Gap detection uses quality scoring 0-100 with constraints weighted highest (25pts), then decisions (15pts), then investigations (10pts)
reason: Constraints are most critical for preventing incorrect agent behavior; investigations are least critical as they're exploratory

content: System Learning Loop uses RecurrenceThreshold=3
reason: 1 occurrence = noise, 2 = coincidence, 3+ = pattern worth addressing - industry standard heuristic

content: Proposal tables use ID/Target/Reason/Approved format for kb-reflect actions
reason: Enables quick orchestrator approval - scan table, mark [x] for approved rows

content: Cross-project agent visibility requires extracting PROJECT_DIR from SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
reason: Beads comments are per-project; to show phase info for agents from other projects, must query the correct project's beads database

content: Cross-project beads queries require PROJECT_DIR from workspace SPAWN_CONTEXT.md
reason: Beads issues are per-project; bd commands run in current directory; for cross-project agent visibility, must extract PROJECT_DIR and query correct project's .beads/ directory

content: cross project agent visibility requires fetching beads comments from agent's project directory, not orchestrator's current directory
reason: Agents spawned with --workdir run in different repos than orchestrator. bd comments uses cwd by default, missing the agent's actual beads issue.

content: Generate gap reasons from context
reason: Gap events contain skill, task, and occurrence data that can be formatted into meaningful kn entry reasons

content: spawn_requires uses *bool for optional boolean fields
reason: Distinguishes unset (nil, use default) from explicitly false; helper Get* methods handle nil gracefully

content: Runtime dependency resolution is preferred over compile-time for cross-directory skill dependencies
reason: skillc's graph validates dependencies exist within the same compilation context; cross-directory deps require orch-go's LoadSkillWithDependencies at spawn time

content: cross project agent visibility requires fetching beads comments from agent's project directory, not orchestrator's current directory
reason: Agents spawned with --workdir run in different repos than orchestrator. bd comments uses cwd by default, missing the agent's actual beads issue.

content: Activity type determines highlighting intensity
reason: Transient states (step-finish) should be visually minimal, while active work (tool, reasoning) deserves subtle attention

content: orch learn clear should be rare - prefer orch learn resolve for individual gaps
reason: Learning loop needs history to detect patterns. Clearing throws away recurring patterns, effectiveness tracking, and trend data. Use resolve for individual items; let 30-day auto-pruning handle the rest.

content: Usage stats consolidation moves reset times to header, removes duplicate stats bar row
reason: Reduces visual clutter while maintaining all information in single location

content: Use shadcn-svelte tooltip component with Tooltip.Provider in layout for SSR
reason: Built on existing bits-ui dependency, no new libs needed, consistent with project patterns

content: Agents modifying web/ files MUST capture visual verification via Playwright MCP before completing
reason: Tests passing does not verify UI renders correctly. Prior instances of UI features shipping with broken rendering despite passing tests.

content: 24 hours is the staleness threshold for orch review
reason: Agents stuck in non-Complete phase for >24h are effectively abandoned. Directory modification time is a reasonable proxy for activity.

content: System resource visibility not needed in orchestrator
reason: External monitoring (sketchybar) already provides CPU/memory visibility; high resource usage in orch processes indicates bugs to fix, not normal states to monitor

content: D.E.K.N. validation gates on synthesis sections only (Knowledge, Next)
reason: Delta and Evidence can be auto-populated from data; only synthesis requires human reflection

content: orch kb ask uses polling instead of SSE streaming for LLM response
reason: SSE streaming via SendMessageWithStreaming returned empty responses. Polling approach checks for completed assistant message in session messages every 500ms with 60s timeout. More reliable for short-lived synthesis queries.

content: RecordResolution must mark ALL matching events, not just most recent
reason: When resolving a gap pattern, all occurrences must be marked or they continue appearing in suggestions

content: Dashboard is_processing visual indicators require status === 'active' check
reason: SSE session.status events may not clear is_processing flag when agent completes, causing stale pulsing animation. Defensive check ensures only active agents show processing state.

content: Systems form around foundational constraints through accumulated compensations - same mechanism as evolution
reason: Observed in orch system: amnesia constraint generated externalization infrastructure, agreement constraint generated governance layer. Not designed top-down, shaped by pressure over time.

content: Mock daemon handlers must run in loop for persistent connections
reason: Client reuses single connection for health check + operations; returning after first request breaks subsequent calls

content: Shell-aware command parsing required for orch learn act
reason: strings.Fields breaks quoted arguments; must use custom parser respecting double/single quotes

content: Mock SSE EventSource with Playwright page.route for load testing
result: EventSource onopen never fires with mocked route response - SSE needs persistent connection

content: Use direct fetch in page.evaluate to test API performance
reason: Bypasses SSE connection requirement while still testing the mock API response time

content: Integration tests should use t.Skip when daemon not available
reason: Allows tests to pass in CI or environments without beads daemon

content: SSE event handlers must check agent status before setting is_processing=true
reason: Late SSE events can arrive after agent status changes, causing stale visual state

content: Use snap for simple visual verification, Playwright MCP for complex UI testing
reason: Playwright MCP tool definitions cost ~5-10k tokens. For basic 'take screenshot' verification in feature-impl, snap CLI is sufficient and nearly zero token cost. Reserve Playwright for: automated assertions, headless CI, complex interactions.

content: Multi-project workspace aggregation uses session.Directory for dynamic discovery
reason: Better than static registry - self-healing, no maintenance, scales naturally with active sessions

content: Could we derive CLAUDE.md empirically from orch learn gaps?
Monitor should delete sessions after completion
reason: Prevents memory leak in long-running daemons - sessions accumulate unboundedly otherwise

content: SSE reconnect should use loop pattern not goroutine spawning
reason: Spawning new goroutines on reconnect leaks goroutines - each reconnection accumulated 2 orphaned goroutines

content: CLI output must be readable at 80 chars (half MacBook screen)
reason: orch status now adapts to terminal width: wide (>120), narrow (80-100), and card (<80) formats

content: bd comment with untracked BeadsID
result: Synthetic IDs like orch-go-untracked-* don't exist in beads database - results in 'issue not found' error

result: Synthetic IDs (format: project-untracked-timestamp) don't exist in beads, so bd comment always fails for --no-track spawns

content: OpenCode handles OAuth auto-refresh via anthropic-auth plugin
reason: Code inspection shows token check at fetch time - no orch implementation needed

content: Ethical questions sometimes dissolve when you find the real question underneath
reason: The ToS violation question dissolved into 'will I ask for what I need?' The surface question was about rules; the real question was about avoiding vulnerability of asking.

content: Surface Before Circumvent: Before working around a constraint, surface it to the people involved
reason: The accountability is a feature, not a cost. Extends Pressure Over Compensation from systems to relationships. Discovered when ToS violation path would have bypassed asking Jim for token budget.

content: OpenCode serve runs via launchd with KeepAlive for auto-restart
reason: Foundation service - orch daemon and serve depend on it. Located at ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.opencode.serve.plist

content: generateReasonFromGaps must ensure 20+ char minimum for kn compatibility
reason: The kn command requires --reason to be at least 20 characters. When gap events lack skill/task metadata, the base reason was too short.

content: Use native Go RPC client for beads integration instead of CLI subprocess
reason: Eliminates subprocess overhead, provides type safety, enables proper error handling. Socket at .beads/bd.sock. See .kb/investigations/2025-12-25-inv-design-beads-integration-strategy-orch.md

content: Dashboard gets lightweight acknowledgment actions (approve, reject, mark reviewed, priority bump); orchestrator keeps reasoning actions (spawn, synthesize, scope)
reason: Control separation: if it requires judgment, orchestrator. If it's confirmation of something already decided, dashboard. Reduces context switching without blurring the thinking/seeing boundary.

content: Land the plane: Transition sessions at ~50-60% context while thinking is still clear, not at 80%+ when scrambling
reason: Handoff quality degrades as context fills. Clean stopping points produce better session resumption than forced stops. Stop while you can still think clearly.

content: Dashboard daemon indicator uses file-based status (daemon-status.json) over IPC
reason: Simple, decoupled approach - daemon writes status to file on each poll, dashboard reads it. No process coupling, works when daemon restarts, allows monitoring from any process.

content: Context waste from debug-retry loops - is fix in orch ecosystem or opencode tool instructions?
Dashboard synthesis review shows synthesis inline with actionable issue creation
reason: Enables orchestrators to act on synthesis recommendations without leaving dashboard UI

content: checking if /api/reflect endpoint needed implementation
result: endpoint was already added in commit 88b71037 along with /api/gaps endpoint - commit message only mentioned gaps but included reflect

content: Issue orch-go-zxy5 had implementation spec but unclear design - agent implemented buttons without review workflow, didn't solve actual problem
result: Feature-impl completed literal spec but missed the point - need design-session first for UX/workflow problems

content: Reactive approach applies to reversible failures; irreversible failures (data loss, state corruption) warrant proactive auditing
reason: Pressure Over Compensation assumes failures are learning signals, but irreversible failures can't teach - the damage is done. Distinguish failure severity when applying the principle.

content: Detection accelerates pressure, prevention relieves it - audits and health checks are detection (surfaces failures faster), not compensation (prevents failures from occurring)
reason: Pressure Over Compensation should reject prevention, not detection. orch doctor is detection - it makes failures visible immediately rather than waiting for downstream breakage. The failure still happens, the learning signal is preserved, but latency is reduced.

content: Reactive infrastructure approach with orch doctor detection
reason: Aligns with pressure-over-compensation principle - failures are signals that tell us which services need KeepAlive, not problems to prevent proactively

content: What artifact type should capture evolving landscape maps (entities, positioning, relationships over time)? Investigations are point-in-time, guides are procedures, decisions are choices. Maps/landscapes are a distinct knowledge type - tracking a domain, not answering a question.
orch review done prompts for synthesis recommendations before closing
reason: Surfaces agent follow-up recommendations to orchestrator during batch completion, preventing value leak

content: Error pattern analysis uses normalized message matching for grouping similar errors
reason: Enables dashboard to show recurring patterns by truncating to 100 chars and trimming whitespace

content: Dashboard queue visibility should use expandable section under stats bar
reason: Consistent with CollapsibleSection pattern, respects 666px constraint, no context switching required

content: orch doctor checks OpenCode (4096), orch serve (3348), and beads daemon
reason: Centralized service health check makes it easy to diagnose infrastructure issues

content: Auto-switch already implemented in account.AutoSwitchIfNeeded and integrated at spawn time in runSpawnWithSkill

content: Global user config in pkg/userconfig reads ~/.orch/config.yaml
reason: Needed unified place for user preferences like notifications. Follows same pattern as pkg/account for ~/.orch/accounts.yaml.

content: How should system detect when new commands/features need skill documentation updates? Current gap: orch-go-iw2i added 'orch doctor' but nothing prompted skill update - orchestrator manually compensated.
Store review state in per-workspace .review-state.json
reason: Enables cleanup when workspace is deleted, keeps state close to synthesis

content: Ready queue UI uses truncate for titles and limit 2 labels shown
reason: Keeps queue items scannable at 666px width

content: Use WithAutoReconnect(3) for beads RPC clients in daemon
reason: Provides resilience against daemon restarts and transient connection issues, matching the pattern already established in pkg/beads

content: Use persistent beads RPC client with WithAutoReconnect for serve.go handlers
reason: Reduces per-request connection overhead and handles daemon restarts gracefully

content: Use WithAutoReconnect(3) for beads RPC client in verify package

content: make install-restart chains install with daemon kickstart

reason: Web validation requires browser control (navigate, wait, interact) before capture - snap only provides capture

content: 5 second debounce for is_processing state
reason: Prevents gold border flashing between rapid tool calls. Agent switches busy/idle every ~100-500ms during active work.

content: Feature-impl light tier is intentional, review tooling needs update
reason: Tier system deliberately separates knowledge-producing skills (full) from implementation-focused skills (light). Review tooling only scans SYNTHESIS.md, creating blind spot for light tier completions.

content: ListSessions returns historical sessions - always use IsSessionActive for liveness checks
reason: OpenCode persists sessions to disk, so session existence doesn't mean it's running

content: Auto-detect new CLI commands by checking git status for Added (A) files in cmd/orch/*.go that contain cobra.Command patterns
reason: Follows existing hasGoChangesInRecentCommits pattern, enables timely documentation prompts

content: Light tier completions detected via .tier file + Phase: Complete in beads comments
reason: SYNTHESIS.md is not produced for light tier spawns, so we need alternative completion detection

content: ReadyQueueSection follows PendingReviewsSection pattern for dedicated dashboard sections
reason: Consistent component architecture, each section handles its own store subscription and toggle state

content: Auto-detect CLI commands feature was already complete
reason: Found detectNewCLICommands at main.go:3082-3150 with tests passing - no additional work needed

content: Use Playwright MCP for web/ visual verification, snap CLI for native macOS capture only
reason: Web validation requires browser control (navigate, wait, interact) before capture - snap only provides capture step

content: bits-ui 1.8.0 uses GroupHeading instead of Label, RadioItem children snippet receives {checked} parameter
reason: Discovered during dropdown-menu component creation - different from older shadcn-svelte examples

content: orch abandon uses same --workdir pattern as spawn for cross-project operations
reason: Consistency with spawn command; beads.DefaultDir enables socket discovery in target project

content: Cross-project beads ID detection uses project prefix comparison
reason: Consistent with abandon command pattern; extracts project from beads ID (e.g., kb-cli from kb-cli-xyz) and compares with current directory name

content: skillc binary is in ~/go/bin
reason: Not in default PATH, needs explicit path or PATH update

content: Verify issue status before starting work
reason: Previous agent completed work (commit 70b2e247) but issue remained in_progress. Check bd show and git log before implementing to avoid duplicate effort.

content: serve.go beads calls already use RPC via verify package
reason: ListOpenIssues, GetIssuesBatch, GetCommentsBatchWithProjectDirs all use beads.NewClient with auto-reconnect internally

content: OpenCode has no /health endpoint - use /session to check server status
reason: OpenCode returns 500 'redirected too many times' for any invalid route including /health. The valid endpoints are /session, /session/{id}, /session/{id}/message, etc.

content: Use generics for retry helper
reason: Retry[T any] pattern enables type-safe retry logic without interface{} casts. Applied to spawn error handling.

content: Surface Before Circumvent mechanism in spawn context uses bd comment CONSTRAINT: prefix for tracking
reason: Provides searchable, structured format for constraint surfacing. Pattern is bd comment <id> 'CONSTRAINT: [what] - [why]'

content: html-to-markdown v2 requires explicit plugin registration (base + commonmark) and WithDomain is a ConvertOptionFunc for ConvertString, not a converter option
reason: v2 API breaking change from v1

content: IsSessionActive should be used for liveness detection, not SessionExists
reason: OpenCode persists sessions to disk, so SessionExists returns true for historical sessions

content: HTTP clients for external APIs should always have timeouts
reason: http.DefaultClient has no timeout which can cause indefinite hangs when servers are unresponsive or in redirect loops

content: Skills with required: true file deliverables need outputs.required; skills with optional deliverables don't
reason: orch complete uses outputs.required to verify file artifacts exist

content: kb extract uses inline lineage metadata (extracted-from, source-project, extraction-date)
reason: Session Amnesia principle requires self-describing artifacts; fresh agents can trace artifact lineage from inline comments

content: kb supersede command uses regex pattern matching to update lineage headers in markdown files
reason: Allows flexible matching of existing header values (empty or populated) and handles files with or without lineage blocks

content: Agent registry removal complete - all state from OpenCode API + beads
reason: Phase 4 complete: removed registry package, documentation cleaned up. No agent-registry.json file needed.

content: skillc is at ~/Documents/personal/skillc/bin/skillc, not in PATH
reason: Found during skill rebuild - binary exists but not symlinked

content: Track fix attempts via events.jsonl scanning
reason: Events.jsonl already logs session.spawned, agent.abandoned, agent.completed with beads_id - no new storage needed for retry pattern detection

content: Visual intent clarification should be spawn-time discipline
reason: Enforcement at spawn prevents ambiguity from entering system; worker phases are optional and late; dedicated agents add overhead without addressing root cause

content: kb reflect --type refine detects kn entries that refine principles using keyword matching (2+ terms required)
reason: Keyword matching is simple, maintainable, and works well with existing kn/kb data structures

content: Theme JSONs use reference resolution pattern (defs + variant objects)
reason: Ported from OpenCode - resolution logic needed for refs to defs, theme properties, and dark/light variants

content: All CLI Makefiles should install to ~/bin, not ~/go/bin
reason: Prevents PATH shadowing - ~/go/bin may have stale binaries with higher PATH priority

content: Playwright MCP extension mode for shared browser
reason: Simplest integration path - human installs extension, agent connects to their actual browser session, no sync infrastructure needed

content: Playwright MCP architecture is near-optimal for agent browser automation
reason: LLM decision loop is the bottleneck (70-90% of time), not MCP protocol (<1%) or browser overhead (5-20%). Accessibility tree approach is 10-100x more efficient than screenshots.

content: 10x browser automation speedup requires agent architecture changes, not protocol optimization
reason: Action batching (3-5x), parallel speculation (2-3x), and action macros (2x for repeated flows) can combine for ~12x improvement. Protocol/browser layers are already optimized.

content: orch complete auto-detects cross-project agents from workspace SPAWN_CONTEXT.md PROJECT_DIR
reason: Follows same pattern as orch abandon --workdir; workspace metadata is authoritative source

content: requestIdleCallback is the right pattern for deferring non-critical UI data fetches in SvelteKit
reason: Browser-native scheduling ensures secondary data doesn't block initial render while still loading promptly

content: Light-tier pending reviews are grouped by default in Swarm Dashboard
reason: 153 identical cards from light-tier spawns were overwhelming - grouping with 'Dismiss All' reduces cognitive load while preserving ability to act on individual items if needed

content: Separate user observations from hypotheses before spawning
reason: Evidence Hierarchy: observations are primary evidence, hypotheses are secondary claims to verify. User saying 'hydration is slow' contains observation (slow) and hypothesis (hydration) that must be parsed.

content: Dylan doesn't interact with dashboard directly - orchestrator uses Glass for all browser interactions

content: UI features require browser verification before marking complete

content: SvelteKit stores using browser APIs must use init() pattern with onMount

content: MCP for agent-internal use, CLI for orchestrator/scripts/humans

content: Glass assert command for orchestrator validation
reason: CLI provides exit codes (0/1) for scripting in orch complete workflow - MCP requires agent context

reason: Investigation found learning.go tracks knowledge gaps, not action outcomes. Self-correction requires observing action→outcome sequences, which are currently transient.

content: Pattern analyzer uses 7-day retention vs 30-day for gap tracking
reason: Behavioral patterns are more ephemeral than knowledge gaps - actions happen frequently, patterns become stale faster

content: Skill changes route by blast radius + change type

content: skillc is in ~/go/bin, not ~/bin

reason: Different from older skillc build workflow
